title: What Makes Flamingos Pink?

scenes.text: Flamingos aren't born pink; they get their color from food!
They feed on algae and crustaceans rich in carotenoids, which turn their feathers pink.
Without a diet rich in these pigments, they would be gray or white.
Their pink coloration is a sign of health and vitality!
scenes.label: SIGN OF HEALTH

scenes.text: Follow for facts that sound fake but aren't.

description: Flamingos owe their pink color to their diet! Discover how their food influences their beauty. Follow for daily facts!
